text: Some population of the cells exhibiting EMT demonstrates the feature of cancer stem cells (CSCs), which are related to cancer malignancy (Shibue & Weinberg, 2017; Shihori Tanabe, 2015a, 2015b; Tanabe, Aoyagi, Yokozaki, & Sasaki, 2015).  EMT phenomenon is related to cancer metastasis and cancer therapy resistance (Smith & Bhowmick, 2016; Tanabe, 2013). The increased expression of enzymes that degrade the extracellular matrix components and the decrease in adhesion to the basement membrane in EMT induces the cell to escape from the basement membrane and metastasis (Smith & Bhowmick, 2016). Morphological changes observed during EMT are associated with therapy resistance (Smith & Bhowmick, 2016).
